Part I Paper Assistant Tool

PAT: AI for Scientific Paper Review

An agentic AI system providing deep feedback on full-length research papers. Deployed in partnership with major conference program committees.

🔍

Mathematical & Logical Errors

Finds errors in proofs, arguments, and derivations

🧪

Experimental Flaws

Points out confounding factors, suggests missing experiments & baselines

📚

Related Work & Clarity

Suggests missing references, improves writing quality

Stage 2: Adaptive Budgeting

Intro, Conclusion
Medium
Methods, Expts

Theory, Proofs

Part II Accelerating Scientific Research with Gemini

Research Across 10+ Fields

Joint work with 30+ collaborators · ICLR & ISIT papers accepted · 20+ researcher testimonials

⭐ Mechanism Design

Extended Revelation Principle to real-valued bids via measure theory

⭐ Online Algorithms

Constructed minimal counterexample (n=3) for Submodular Welfare

Approximation Algorithms

Max-Cut: cross-pollinated with Stone-Weierstrass Theorem

Complexity Theory

Search vs Decision in S₂P — "vibe-proving" with AI IDE

Computational Geometry

Steiner Trees ↔ Kirszbraun Extension Theorem

Cryptography

SNARGs from LWE: found fatal flaw via iterative self-correction

Query Complexity

Generalized lower bounds from 2 to t rounds

Streaming Algorithms

Entropy estimation: Õ(√n) → polylog(n) state changes

Information Theory

Courtade-Kumar: AI-generated paper, strong accept at ISIT

Robust Coresets

k-means: eliminated last log factor — optimal bounds

Physics

Cosmic String Spectra: neuro-symbolic verification loop

ML Optimization

Gumbel Sigmoid: variance acts as L₀ relaxation

8 / 13
Part II Deep Dive

Example: Extending the Revelation Principle

The Problem

The Revelation Principle — a cornerstone of mechanism design — traditionally assumes countable or rational bid spaces. Extending to real-valued bids (ℝ) breaks standard proof techniques.

What AI Did

Gemini autonomously introduced measure-theoretic tools to bypass the countability barrier — cross-pollinating from a different mathematical branch.

Why It Matters

A foundational result for the EC community. AI's cross-pollination enabled a proof that had eluded prior work.

Classical

Countable bids · ℚ or finite sets

↓

🤖 AI Cross-Pollination

Measure Theory Tools

↓

Extended ✓

Real-valued bids · ℝ — uncountable

9 / 13
Part II Key Lessons

Methodology & Lessons Learned

📈

Inference Scaling

More model calls → higher P(good solution). Some problems: 1 out of N calls succeeds for very large N.

🎭

Distinct Personalities

Separate idea generator and verifier. Avoid bias — don't say "prove" or "disprove."

✅

External Verification

Python checks, search, Lean formal proofs — far less hallucination with external tools.

🎓

"Vibe-Proving"

Human as high-level orchestrator, AI as strong grad student. Generates proofs, self-corrects.

🔄

PAT as Automated Verifier

Feed research output back to PAT — closing the generation → verification loop.

💡

Key Insight

"The bottleneck shifts from generation to verification."

10 / 13
Part III Multi-Agent Collaboration

Teamwork: Building an OS with 93 Agents

Shifting from synchronous, chat-style interaction to asynchronous, multi-agent teamwork

The Challenge: build a functional operating system from scratch capable of running FreeDoom — spanning kernel, process & memory management, filesystem, video & keyboard drivers.

93
autonomous subagents
15K+
model calls
2.6B+
tokens processed
~12h
to completion

Launched at Google I/O 2026 in Antigravity 2.0

Source: antigravity.google/blog/google-antigravity-built-an-os

11 / 13

Teamwork Demo: Building an OS from Scratch

12 / 13
Part III Architecture

Seven Specialized Agent Roles

🛎️
Sentinel
The "front-desk manager" of the project that does not write code, analyze logs, or make technical decisions. It focuses entirely on structuring user intent, spawning the Orchestrator, and supervising overall task completion.
🎯
Orchestrator
A dispatch-only manager, i.e. never writes code or executes builds itself. Focuses on decomposing requirements into milestones, kicking off other specialized subagents, and synthesizing reports.
🔭
Explorer
Analyzes requirements and previous logs to write formal strategies for the Orchestrator to act on, but never writes code itself.
⚒️
Worker
The actual coder that implements the strategies, builds the code, and runs tests.
👀
Reviewer
Independently reviews the Worker's changes for design correctness, edge cases, and interface contract compliance.
⚡
Critic
Stress-tests the solution, runs adversarial tests to find gaps in coverage.
🔍
Auditor
An independent investigator that verifies the authenticity and robustness of the generated solutions.

Looking Ahead

"Working with agents asynchronously really requires a different user experience than synchronous workflows."

Open Questions

"Will this decrease how much developers test and verify the code that they ship?"

Future

"What happens as the models invariably get even more intelligent, do we need to rethink this whole orchestration all over again?"

Source: antigravity.google/blog/google-antigravity-built-an-os
